title: P2 Operator Visibility: Tasks #10 + #11
goal: Implement dogfood backlog items #10 (run-level merged live output) and #11 (compact live monitor/status command), each in an isolated worktree with two sequential review-and-fix passes.

decisions.title: Per-task worktree isolation
decisions.rationale: Each task (#10 and #11) runs in its own git worktree branch so implementation and review edits never conflict across tasks. The worktree merges back after both reviewers pass.

decisions.title: Two sequential review-and-fix passes per task
decisions.rationale: Each task gets two reviewers who independently review AND fix issues. Reviewer A uses @glm51 for semantic/logic depth. Reviewer B uses @cx-reviewer for standards/coverage breadth. Both loop until their review returns PASS. Sequential within a worktree to avoid edit conflicts.

decisions.title: Pipeline across tasks, chain within tasks
decisions.rationale: Tasks #10 and #11 are independent and launch in parallel worktrees. Within each task, impl -> review-A -> review-B is sequential because they share a worktree and review-and-fix implies edits.

phases.name: task-10-live-output
phases.title: Task #10: Run-level Merged Live Output
phases.goal: Add run-level live output that merges all agent streams with stable colored agent-name prefixes.

jobs.name: impl-10
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc worker. Do not run ccc or spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Implement dogfood backlog item #10: Run-level merged live output.

What to build:
- Add run-level live output that merges all live agent streams with stable colored agent-name prefixes.
- The merged view should interleave output from multiple running agents, each line prefixed with the agent name in a distinct color.
- Integrate into the TUI run detail view (workflow_tui_render.py make_run_detail).
- Add tests in tests/test_workflow.py.

Code standards:
- No file over 2000 LOC (hard), flag over 1000 (soft).
- No duplicated logic.
- Meaningful names, no magic numbers.
- One module, one responsibility.

After implementing, run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow

Commit with message: feat(workflow): add run-level merged live output (#10)

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|blocked), summary (string), files_changed (array), tests_passed (bool), commit (string or null).

jobs.name: review-10a
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @glm51 review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Review-and-fix pass for Task #10 (run-level merged live output). The implementation was just committed in this worktree.

Review focus:
1. Semantic correctness -- does the merged output correctly interleave streams? Are agent-name prefixes stable? Are colors distinct?
2. Edge cases -- what happens with 0 agents? 1 agent? Agent that produces no output? Agent that fails mid-stream?
3. State lifecycle -- does the merged view update correctly as agents start/stop/complete?
4. Test coverage -- are the new features tested? Are edge cases covered?
5. No behavioral regression in existing tests.

Review-and-fix loop:
- Review the code and run tests.
- If you find concrete bugs, fix them narrowly and commit: fix(workflow): address glm51 review for #10
- If no bugs, do not commit.
- Re-run tests after any fix.
- Repeat until your review returns PASS.

Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array of {severity, file, line, issue, fix}), tests_passed (bool), commits (array), notes.

jobs.name: review-10b
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @cx-reviewer review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Review-and-fix pass for Task #10 (run-level merged live output). A previous semantic review has already been applied in this worktree.

Review focus:
1. Code standards -- no file over 2000 LOC, no duplication, meaningful names, consistent error handling.
2. Test quality -- are tests deterministic? Do they use fixtures? Are snapshot tests up to date?
3. Re-export completeness -- are all new public symbols re-exported from workflow_tui.py?
4. Integration -- does the merged live output work with the existing TUI app (workflow_tui_app.py)?
5. Check the previous reviewer's fixes -- are they correct? Do they introduce new issues?

Review-and-fix loop:
- Review the code and run tests.
- If you find concrete issues, fix them narrowly and commit: fix(workflow): address cx-reviewer review for #10
- If no issues, do not commit.
- Re-run tests after any fix.
- Repeat until your review returns PASS.

Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array of {severity, file, line, issue, fix}), tests_passed (bool), commits (array), notes.

phases.name: task-11-compact-status
phases.title: Task #11: Compact Live Monitor/Status Command
phases.goal: Add a low-noise workflow status/watch view with one compact row per run/agent.

jobs.name: impl-11
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc worker. Do not run ccc or spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Implement dogfood backlog item #11: Compact live monitor/status command.

What to build:
- Add a low-noise `workflow status` / `workflow watch` view for lead agents and humans.
- One compact row per run/agent with: status, PID, elapsed time, last event, latest output excerpt, tool-call count, token delta.
- Optional --json output.
- Avoid direct run.json dumps and broad log tails during active runs.
- Avoid printing full process command lines by default (worker prompts are huge); show only PID/process group, runner type, elapsed time, and safe command basename unless --verbose.
- Support long quiet waits without repeatedly opening helper sessions.
- Add to the wf CLI wrapper (scripts/wf).
- Add tests in tests/test_workflow.py.

Code standards:
- No file over 2000 LOC (hard), flag over 1000 (soft).
- No duplicated logic.
- Meaningful names, no magic numbers.
- One module, one responsibility.

After implementing, run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow

Commit with message: feat(workflow): add compact live monitor/status command (#11)

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|blocked), summary (string), files_changed (array), tests_passed (bool), commit (string or null).

jobs.name: review-11a
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @glm51 review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Review-and-fix pass for Task #11 (compact live monitor/status command). The implementation was just committed in this worktree.

Review focus:
1. Semantic correctness -- does the status command show accurate, current data? Is the compact format readable?
2. Edge cases -- what happens with no runs? Paused runs? Runs with no agents? Very long prompts?
3. Output discipline -- does --json produce valid JSON? Does --verbose actually expand the right fields?
4. CLI integration -- does the wf wrapper correctly route to the new command?
5. Test coverage -- are the new features tested? Edge cases?

Review-and-fix loop:
- Review the code and run tests.
- If you find concrete bugs, fix them narrowly and commit: fix(workflow): address glm51 review for #11
- If no bugs, do not commit.
- Re-run tests after any fix.
- Repeat until your review returns PASS.

Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array of {severity, file, line, issue, fix}), tests_passed (bool), commits (array), notes.

jobs.name: review-11b
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as a ccc @cx-reviewer review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent. Work in the current cwd.

Review-and-fix pass for Task #11 (compact live monitor/status command). A previous semantic review has already been applied in this worktree.

Review focus:
1. Code standards -- no file over 2000 LOC, no duplication, meaningful names, consistent error handling.
2. Test quality -- are tests deterministic? Do they avoid flakiness?
3. CLI ergonomics -- is the command discoverable? Does --help document the new flags?
4. Check the previous reviewer's fixes -- are they correct? Do they introduce new issues?
5. Does the status command avoid opening helper sessions or spawning subprocesses during long waits?

Review-and-fix loop:
- Review the code and run tests.
- If you find concrete issues, fix them narrowly and commit: fix(workflow): address cx-reviewer review for #11
- If no issues, do not commit.
- Re-run tests after any fix.
- Repeat until your review returns PASS.

Run: WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 -m unittest tests.test_workflow

Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array of {severity, file, line, issue, fix}), tests_passed (bool), commits (array), notes.
